answer:
\boxed{C}

The transport layer in the OSI model is responsible for end-to-end communication and error recovery, with **TCP (Transmission Control Protocol)** and **UDP (User Datagram Protocol)** being its primary protocols. Options A (HTTP/FTP) and D (SMTP) are application-layer protocols, while B (TCP/IP) incorrectly pairs a transport-layer protocol with a network-layer one.
